mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, which positively drives S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment is a key coincident indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a potential recessionary shock, increasing risk aversion and expectations of lower future earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank is raising the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also accelerating, the combination of higher discount rates and potential margin compression creates a strongly negative environment for equity valuations.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal does not remain statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals a cyclical rebound in manufacturing and business investment, leading to upward revisions in earnings expectations and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a pro-cyclical indicator.
failure_condition: The mechanism's positive directional accuracy is below 55% during periods identified as post-shock recovery (e.g., 2009-2011, 2020-2021).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an unexpected weakening of consumer spending, a primary driver of the US economy, leading to immediate negative revisions in GDP and earnings forecasts.
candidate_trigger: Real RSAFS (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) month-over-month change < -1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: Triggered signals are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns on average (mean return >= 0) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is exceptionally low, the resulting low discount rate for future cash flows and lack of viable yield alternatives in fixed income (TINA effect) pushes capital into equities, supporting higher valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC and post-COVID ZIRP periods saw strong equity performance.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% is not statistically higher than when FEDFUNDS >= 1.0% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of broad economic strength, reflecting consumer confidence and driving activity in multiple related sectors, thus positively impacting expected corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a key interest-rate sensitive and cyclical sector.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 12-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment, which compresses corporate profit margins and reduces investor risk appetite, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth decelerates while CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth accelerates
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a poor period for equities.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.
